supporting_quotes: Two people were killed and one sustained serious injuries as a BMW car rammed into a divider and then overturned multiple times on the Mumbai-Vadodara Highway in Mumbai’s Badlapur in the early hours of Sunday.
The incident took place at around 4 am on a stretch of the Mumbai-Vadodara highway near Badlapur when the victims were returning from Titwala.
the deceased, identified as Yogesh Dighe (26) and Rikeba Jacob (24), were residents of Badlapur

field_updates.title: Badlapur BMW Z4 Crash
field_updates.accused_gap: No named accused, arrests, or prosecution details were identified in the frozen source set.
field_updates.summary: News18 reported that a BMW Z4 rammed into a divider and overturned multiple times on the Mumbai-Vadodara Highway near Badlapur in the early hours of 21 June 2026, killing two people and seriously injuring one. The article identified the deceased as Badlapur residents Yogesh Dighe and Rikeba Jacob, said a case of accidental death was registered, and noted that police were checking whether the driver was under the influence of alcohol.

compensation.paid_note: No compensation reporting was frozen for this draft.

timeline.event: News18 reported that two people were killed and one person was seriously injured when a BMW Z4 crashed into a divider and overturned multiple times on the Mumbai-Vadodara Highway near Badlapur in the early hours of 21 June 2026.

The article said the crash happened around 4 am on a stretch of the Mumbai-Vadodara highway near Badlapur, that the BMW Z4 was allegedly being driven at 250 kmph, and that the deceased were identified as Yogesh Dighe (26) and Rikeba Jacob (24), both Badlapur residents.

News18 said a case of accidental death was registered and that police were investigating all aspects of the crash, including whether the driver was under the influence of alcohol; a forensic team collected evidence from the site.
